races.name: Classic Velox Adélie de Vitré
races.description: The 30th edition of the Breton classic, and 4th round of the FDJ United Series / Coupe de France 2026. 174.2km in circuits entirely around Vitré: 5 laps of the 21.1km outer circuit then 8 laps of the 8.9km finishing circuit, making for a relentlessly punchy day on rolling Ille-et-Vilaine roads. The Côte de la Chênelière (880m @ 4%) features with ~6km to go on the final laps and acts as the springboard for late attacks. 19 teams: 3 WorldTour (Decathlon CMA CGM, Groupama-FDJ United, Lotto-Intermarché), 10 ProTeam, 5 Continental. Defending champion: Stian Edvardsen-Fredheim (Uno-X Mobility, 2025).

broadcasts.notes: HBO Max France coverage from 14:00 CEST (12:00 UTC).
NOVO 19 and Eurosport 2 from 14:30 CEST (12:30 UTC). NOVO 19 is the official TV home of the FDJ United Series / Coupe de France.
Eurosport 2 across Europe from 14:30 CEST / 12:30 UTC.

data_confidence.notes: Official start 12:15 CEST / 10:15 UTC confirmed by DirectVelo (fictif 12:10, réel 12:15) and PCS LiveStats feed. Distance: DirectVelo and Le Mag du Cyclisme confirm 174.2km; TotalVelo and 3bikes cite 174.5km — using 174.2km per official French sources and DirectVelo. 30th edition confirmed by DirectVelo. Format: 5 × 21.1km + 8 × 8.9km = 175.7km total circuits (slight variance likely from neutral/transfer km). 19 teams confirmed by Le Mag du Cyclisme (3 WT + 10 Pro + 5 CT). Broadcast: HBO Max France from 14:00 CEST; NOVO 19 / Eurosport 2 from 14:30 CEST — two separate French sources confirm. No UK or North American broadcaster identified for this 1.1 race. Favourites: Jeannière (TotalEnergies, podium 2025), Menten (Lotto-Intermarché), Venturini (Unibet Rose Rockets, fresh from La Roue Tourangelle win), Penhoët (Groupama-FDJ, podium 2025), Gautherat (Decathlon, won Paris-Camembert Tuesday).

calendar_notes.no_womens_11_today: No women's 1.1 or WWT races on April 3. The next significant women's race is Tour of Flanders Women (WWT Monument, April 5).

issues: Distance minor variance: 174.2km (DirectVelo, Le Mag) vs 174.5km (TotalVelo, 3bikes). Using 174.2km — DirectVelo has consistently been accurate on French race distances in this session.
No UK/international broadcaster confirmed for this race beyond Eurosport 2 / HBO Max France. The FDJ United Series is primarily a French domestic series and international coverage is limited.
Grand Prix Alanya (Turkey, 1.2) is also on today but below the 1.1 threshold requested and excluded.
Race has not yet started at file generation time (10:15 UTC start).

recent_results.notes: Fastest-ever edition (48.5kph avg). Ganna overhauled solo Van Aert with ~100m to go after multiple mechanicals earlier in the race. Heartbreak again for Van Aert.

recent_results.race: Dwars door Vlaanderen – Women (WWT, first year)
recent_results.notes: Photo finish. Reusser's first race back after UAE Tour injury. Reusser attacked at 24km, Vollering bridged. Nooijen nearly stole it in the final 200m but the duo responded.
All-French podium. Six-man breakaway held on; Gautherat attacked in final kilometre.

upcoming.notes: Navarra hills, last 1.Pro before Flanders weekend. UK: HBO Max; US/CA: FloBikes.
1.1 race in Belgium. Both men's and women's editions.

upcoming.notes: The biggest race of the cobbled spring. Pogačar vs Van der Poel. Evenepoel confirmed shock debut. UK: HBO Max/TNT; US/CA: FloBikes; AU: SBS.
Kopecky, Reusser, Vollering, Wiebes, Pieterse headline. UK: HBO Max; US/CA: FloBikes; AU: SBS.
